Franz von Papen?
An relatively unknown Catholic Conservative from the <span style="color: #000000;">Center Party</span>. Schleicher's preferred candidate.
With Goerdeler and Westarp out of the equation, Hindenburg has grasped the straw Schleicher offered and appointed a far-right Catholic Conservative, Franz von Papen, from the <span style="color: #000000;">Center Party</span>, as chancellor. Papen is relatively unknown, only whose political experience is limited to two terms in the Prussian Landtag. The two have been friends since their days in the Prussian military academy.
Papen's decision to take the chancellorship, despite 
's urging and Papen's promise not to, Hindenburg has used the same tactic on him as he used on BrÃ¼ning, convincing him to accept the position. Due to Papen breaking his promise to 
 just after he made it, expulsion proceedings and against him are interrupted by his resignation from the <span style="color: #000000;">Center Party</span>, forever ruining his relations with the party necessary for a parliamentary majority as they strongly denounce him as the "Ephialtes of the <span style="color: #000000;">Center Party</span>".
It's unknown of the exact reasons why Schleicher would choose a man like Papen as BrÃ¼ning's successor, but we can assume he was trying to secure the support of the <span style="color: #000000;">Center Party</span> and <span style="color: #7A3C00;">Nazis</span>, as he's a far-right member of the former, and that Papen would be an easy person to control.
Time will tell if the <span style="color: #7A3C00;">
NSDAP
</span> will honor its promises to Schleicher, which they'd purposefully refused to write on paper.
BrÃ¼ning's Race against Time
Hindenburg's inner circle is conspiring against him...
BrÃ¼ning's Race against Time
BrÃ¼ning's days are numbered. The only reason President Hindenburg is keeping him around is solely by the fact of his foreign policy expertise, and his claim that he's only 'one hundred meters from the finish line' from achieving a huge breakthrough in terms of securing an abolishment of reparations at Lausanne and Germany's rearmament at Geneva, an issue close to Hindenburg's heart. The only hope for BrÃ¼ning's political salvation rests in those conferences. 
There is also no precedent of the President sacking a government that still enjoys a parliamentary majority. 
However, General Kurt von Schleicher sees BrÃ¼ning as the biggest obstacle in his '<span class="tooltip-text" title="Coined by conservative elites like Schleicher and Papen, this strategy aims to control the dynamic Nazi movement and integrate it into the state by introducing the party to government. In their eyes, this would moderate the Nazis, transforming them into a constructive component of the state.">taming strategy</span>', as BrÃ¼ning has refused to take serious efforts toward bringing the <span style="color: #7A3C00;">national</span> <span style="color: #3E88B3;">opposition</span> into government. The sense of urgency Schleicher has invoked onto the issue, along with his convincing of Hindenburg's State Secretary Otto Meissner and his son Oskar, has brought Hindenburg to make an early and rash decision on the matter.
We are once again compelled to vote in favor of the 
 government to prevent the extremist parties on the <span style="color: #7A3C00;">far</span>-<span style="color: #3E88B3;">right</span> and <span style="color: #700000;">left</span> from translating their growing popular support into parliamentary gains. Some members of the <span style="color: #C0A054;">
DVP
</span>'s left wing, such as Curtius and Otto Thiel, have also voted with the government or abstained, going against their wider party.
Despite the new right-wing cabinet, the <span style="color: #7A3C00;">national</span> <span style="color: #3E88B3;">opposition</span> is still refusing to support BrÃ¼ning, as they want all the power to themselves.
The Reichstag convenes.

Chancellor 
 Sacked
President Hindenburg has made his dissatisfaction with the <span style="color: #c00000;">socialist</span> presence in government clear and has used his presidential powers to remove us by force. It seems like this decision wasn't a rash one, but rather a matter of finding the appropriate pretext to dismiss our government.
The <span style="color: #c00000;">SPD</span> remaining electorally resilient despite the radicalizing effects of the economic depression has thwarted Hindenburgâs plan to burden us with governance and erode our popularity. Realizing his strategy has failed, he now moves to dismiss us from government to prevent further <span style="color: #c00000;">socialist</span> reforms from fueling our rising support. 
Germany's economic crisis and our inadequate response have severely tested Hindenburg's patience, as he favors an austerity-driven approach. 
The rapid rise of the <span style="color: #7A3C00;">far</span>-<span style="color: #3E88B3;">right</span> in local elections has pushed Hindenburg to appoint a government more in line with the national mood. 
Kurt von Schleicher and the Reichswehr, outraged by our actions, have leveraged their influence over Hindenburg to push for our removal. 
Business leaders, frustrated by our policies, have pressured Hindenburgâs inner circle to act against us. 
This is a blatant violation of democracy, but we are powerless to stop it. Our only hope is to rally enough support in the next election to reclaim our mandate.

</span>, which all state governments are expected to enforce. We have already noticed a significant drop in political violence, and this decree has dealt a strong blow to the <span style="color: #7A3C00;">Nazis</span>, who have relied on intimidation to gain votes.

SA
</span> were leaked by none other than General Kurt von Schleicher, who believes this move interferes with his "<span class="tooltip-text" title="Coined by conservative elites like Schleicher and Papen, this strategy aims to control the dynamic Nazi movement and integrate it into the state by introducing the party to government. In their eyes, this would moderate the Nazis, transforming them into a constructive component of the state.">taming strategy</span>." 
He and the Reichswehr have submitted complaints to President Hindenburg, arguing that the accusations used to justify the <span style="color: #7A3C00;">

</span> ban equally apply to the Reichsbanner. The file presented to Hindenburg consists mostly of clippings from right-wing newspapers and a Reichsbanner training manual. Even Groener finds these charges groundless, but Schleicher wields significant influence over the aging president, who now believes that the ban must be extended to the Reichsbanner.

Schleicher's Schemes
Chancellor Schleicher claims to have a plan to save Germany...
Schleicher's Schemes
Chancellor Kurt von Schleicher claims to have a plan to save Germany, and he has reached out to the Social Democrats with vague promises. He claims that in order to govern, the Reichstag must be temporarily suspended to prevent the Nazis and Communists from disrupting government, in effect giving him and Hindenburg dictatorial powers. In the meantime, he is enacting a public works program and job creation plan, which he claims will stabilize the economy,
The trade unions are somewhat favorable to Schleicher's plan, as they see a commonality between Schleicher's economic plan and the ADGB's WTB-plan for debt-funded job creation. However, the rest of our party is vehemently against Schleicher. 
Rumors are that Schleicher is also reaching out to the Nazis with similar promises of cooperation; he seems to believe that he can "tame" the Nazis. Most in our party do not trust him to not bring the Nazis to power. In addition, working with him would essentially mean the end of our claim to be a "democratic" party.

Young Plan Referendum
While the Young Plan seems to be a positive development for the country, the Right are gathering signatures to initiate a referendum this December. While the referendum itself is likely to fail, their propaganda is striking a chord with many, especially amid the ongoing depression.
It comes down to us to campaign against the rhetoric of those that seek to destroy our republic. 
We are able to use government resources for this campaign, lowering the cost for our party.
Point out the gains of the <span style="color: #7A3C00;">
NSDAP
</span>, who have capitalized on our lack of a program.
The party still doesn't view the <span style="color: #7A3C00;">NSDAP</span> as a significant enemy, nor take them seriously.

</span> and <span style="color: #7A3C00;">

</span> have opened the party's eyes, and they are now ready to support our plan as a means to present a solution for the workers.
With our hard work, we have finally convinced the majority of the party to adopt the WTB plan! 
We can begin implementing the plan as soon as possible, as long as we have the Economic and Finance ministries.

The WTB Plan
Wladimir Woytinsky and the ADGB have presented a proposal.
Woytinsky is promising the workers false hope, retain our current policy.

The WTB Plan
Following Woytinsky's announcement of intentions to draft a national action plan to stimulate the economy through extensive public works, he, Tarnow, and Baade have presented the WTB Plan, named after them, to the ADGB and <span style="color: #c00000;">
SPD
</span> leadership. While the ADGB has shown enthusiasm and formally adopted the plan, the <span style="color: #c00000;">
SPD
</span>'s economic expert, Rudolf Hilferding, along with Rudolf Breitscheid and party leader Otto Wels, are much more resistant, showing significant skepticism.
The fear of inflation caused by funding the plan through public works, especially with hyperinflation still fresh in memory, and the unwillingness to "play doctor at the bedside of capitalism," are the main reasons for the Centrists' reluctance. Maybe they can be convinced?

We have increased our campaigning among the unemployed. 
The welfare cuts and resultant poverty make our campaign more difficult, since we are associated with the government that enacted these cuts, and our campaign may even backfire. 
Adopting the WTB plan gives us a new rallying point around which to attract support from the unemployed.
We attempt to convince the party to support a program of mass nationalizations in response to the economic crisis. Leftists argue that this policy is in accordance with our long-standing <span style="color: #c00000;">socialist</span> platform, while reformists and some trade unionists argue that the plan is unrealistic, and that we need to focus on more immediate improvements to workers' lives.
We have not yet formally adopted the plan. More work needs to be done.
Our anti-bourgeois rhetoric is popular among the left, but may make it harder to build the coalitions that are necessary for government in the future.

High Inflation
Workers are seeing their purchasing power decline.
Raise taxes.
Reduce welfare and social spending.
Impose price and wage controls.
Surely the Reichsbank has a plan this time?
Realistically, there is nothing we can do.
High Inflation
Inflation has risen. People of all social classes are seeing their purchasing power fall. Memories of the hyperinflation period of 1923 echo through the public conscious, even though the present inflation is not nearly as severe. The bourgeois parties and their laissez-faire platforms stand to gain from this, to a lesser degree the <span style="color: #7A3C00;">Nazis</span>.
The people and the press are clamoring for action to reduce inflation.

Subending A - One and a Half Party system
The <span style="color: #c00000;">
SPD
</span>'s rule over Germany has endured for decades, following its sublime management of the Great Depression and successful expansion of support beyond the urban working class. To this day, the <span style="color: #c00000;">
SPD
</span> retains its status as the "natural governing party," having only been out of office during brief stints in times of crisis. Only a united coalition of all parties from the <span style="color: #FF5349;">

</span> to the <span style="color: #3E88B3;">
DNVP
</span> has ever managed to take powerâonly to quickly collapse, paving the way for yet more years of <span style="color: #c00000;">

</span> rule.
Demographic Groups
Working Class
: The urban working class - blue-collar workers in factories, workshops, mines, and the like. Most are <span style="color: #c00000;">socialists</span>, supporting either the <span style="color: #c00000;">
SPD
</span> or <span style="color: #700000;">
KPD
</span>. They make up 40-50% of the population.
Old Mittelstand
: The old middle class consists of independent artisans, shopkeepers, and small-time businesspeople. They tend to be conservative and are the strongest base for the <span style="color: #7A3C00;">far</span>-<span style="color: #3E88B3;">right</span>. 10-15% of the population.
New Mittelstand
: The new middle class consists of white-collar workers and government employees. This is the base of <span style="color: #D3C24D;">liberalism</span>. 10-15% of the population.
Rural
: Small and large farmers and independent rural workers. Protestant farmers usually supports the 
<span style="color: #3E88B3;">

Economic Policy
Next month

<p class="both">
It is a bright time for German democracy. After a period of struggle, inflation is finally under control, and the economy is expanding steadily. In foreign policy, we have reached agreements with the Western powers and the <span style="color: #750e0e;">Soviet Union</span> that might make genuine peace a reality. <br><br> Now, the task of <span style="color: #c00000;">Social Democracy</span> is to prepare for the next election. The <span style="color: #000000;">bourgeois</span>-<span style="color: #3E88B3;">right</span> government of Wilhelm Marx is unpopular, and we stand to gain. Perhaps the next government will have a <span style="color: #c00000;">socialist</span> majority. 
</p>

<p class="other">
Austria's constitution has been amended to grant the President increased powers, including the authority to dismiss parliament, along with introducing direct presidential elections by the people. This has transformed Austria into a semi-presidential system of government, not too dissimilar to our own. The amendment was a result of a compromise with the <span style="color: #000000;">Christian Social Party</span>'s push for a more authoritarian and centralized government, as they needed the <span style="color: #C8102E;">Austrian Social Democrats</span>' approval to pass the amendment.

</span> cells within the Reichswehr. Under normal circumstances, Hitler's statementsâtantamount to preparing for high treasonâwould have led to prosecution. Instead, the authorities are tolerating, even legitimizing the <span style="color: #7A3C00;">Nazi</span> agenda.

<p class="other">
BrÃ¼ningâs decision to sideline the Reichstag and its parties from any meaningful role in shaping his fiscal and economic policies severely undermined the legitimacy of the bourgeois parties in the eyes of their traditional electorates, due to their powerlessness to dismantle his more repugnant policies. His deflationary and austerity measures deepened the economic crisis, radicalizing the very social groups upon which these moderate parties relied for electoral support. This has led to heightened tensions between BrÃ¼ning and his supporting parties, some of whom are even threatening to abandon his coalition.

<p class="other">
Von Schleicher's bluffsâsuch as proposing a cabinet that includes both Stegerwald and Hugenbergâhave lost all credibility. <p class="other">
With progress stalled across nearly all sectors, Schleicher has attempted to shift focus by bringing the issue of rearmament back into the public eye. </p>
<p class="dnvp">
Having failed to extract concessions from the chancellor to turn Germany into a dictatorship, the more radical elements within his party have pressured him into adopting an all-out opposition to Schleicher. Furthermore, his initiative to restart peasant homesteads on bankrupt eastern estates has drawn the ire of conservative elites. With the next vote of no confidence looming, it may well come down to Schleicher versus the entire chamber. 
</p>

Women's Rights
According to the Constitution, women are equal to men, but this is not followed in practice. Women in the workforce are not paid equally for the same work, in addition to often being pushed out of employment after marriage. Men are still favored in marriage and family policy, and there are restrictions on women's rights in many other domains. And that is not to mention the overwhelming patriarchal culture present in Germany.
For better or for worse, the <span style="color: #c00000;">Socialists</span> stand virtually alone in supporting increased rights for women, and even within the party, the leadership does not consider it a priority. 
Unfortunately, women still disproportionately support the bourgeois parties.

Banking Crisis
The DarmstÃ¤dter und Nationalbank, or Danat Bank, has collapsed! Along with it goes much of the German banking system. Transport Strike in Berlin
In the lead-up to the upcoming federal elections, a strike has been organized in response to the wage cuts negotiated between the BVG (Berlin Transport Company) and our own <span style="color: #c00000;">socialist</span> union, the Gesamtverband. Although the wage cuts have been reduced by roughly 90% due to the Gesamtverband's efforts, the <span style="color: #700000;">

</span> (the <span style="color: #700000;">

The <span style="color: #700000;">

</span> is not protesting a modest reduction in the BVG workersâ relatively high wagesâemployees also benefit from company housing and sports clubsâbut is seeking a political strike instead. Despite having a smaller share of workers, the <span style="color: #700000;">
RGO
</span> holds a stronger position due to the formation of "unity committees," which they dominate. Now, a newly formed strike committee that includes the Gesamtverband and the <span style="color: #7A3C00;">
NSBO
</span>, the <span style="color: #7A3C00;">Nazi</span> union, is set to vote on whether to proceed with the strike.

</span> and <span style="color: #7A3C00;">Nazi</span> propaganda spreading throughout the workforce, our local union is left with no choice but to hold a vote among the entire workforce, rather than just union members.

Change the structure and funding of education.
Do not change school funding or organization.
Education in Germany has traditionally been organized on religious and class lines. Schools are often either Protestant or Catholic (or rarely <span style="color: #005EB8;">Jewish</span>), and religious classes are required in most mixed-denomination schools. In addition, the working class has at most eight years of public education in primary and middle schools, while the middle and upper classes have access to private schools and Gymnasiums that prepare students for universities. Thus, the universities tend to be dominated by <span style="color: #3E88B3;">conservatives</span> and <span style="color: #808080;">reactionaries</span>, which has negative consequences for bureaucratic recruitment.
The ban is enthusiastically carried out by the police, perhaps too enthusiastically. However, the <span style="color: #000000;">moderate</span> and <span style="color: #C0A054;">center-right</span> parties approve, as do the police.

Internal Security

Austrian Customs Union
The 
 foreign minister has a proposal for a customs union with Austria. We can either accept or reject this agreement. 
The government of Chancellor 
 has a proposal for a customs union with Austria. Most likely, this will happen whatever we decide. 
The nationalist right has long yearned for closer ties with Austria, with the eventual goal of the merger of the two German-speaking countries. A customs union with Austria is the first step towards this goal. Additionally, many believe that a union with Austria would improve our current economic circumstances.
However, the Allies, especially <span style="color: #002654;">France</span>, are likely to object. This agreement would break the terms of the Versailles Treaty, and could lead to economic sanctions, further hurting our economy.

Ending 4
Following a brief civil war between <span style="color: #000000;">rep</span><span style="color: #DD0000;">ubl</span><span style="color: #FFCC00;">ican</span> forces and the <span style="color: #7A3C00;">far</span>-<span style="color: #3E88B3;">right</span>, weâve been given a blank slate to rebuild Germany on a truly democratic and social foundationâfree, for now, from the fear of reactionary reprisals. A minor coup attempt by General Bredow is foiled, and with it, the Reichswehr is finally brought under the banner of the <span style="color: #000000;">rep</span><span style="color: #DD0000;">ubl</span><span style="color: #FFCC00;">ic</span>.
But it is not all sunshine and rainbows. The civil war has devastated countless lives, and the costs of reconstruction and welfare have triggered the return of hyperinflationary nightmares. Hunger now exceeds levels seen during the Great Depression, and many are already growing disillusioned with their supposed saviors. Though the reactionary and far-right forces were defeated on the battlefield, it's only a matter of years before a new right-wing movement emerges and takes root among the weary and discontented populace.
No war as destructive as the Great War would come again. Slowly, Germany and the <span style="color: #0A3161;">Western</span> <span style="color: #C8102E;">Allies</span> began to rebuild relations, but the <span style="color: #002654;">French</span> reoccupation of the Rhineland has complicated efforts. <span style="color: #7A3C00;">Fascism</span> never takes root as a dominant force, and the primary threat to European democracy becomes the resurgent <span style="color: #750e0e;">Soviet Union</span>. The Spanish Civil War ends in 1936 with a Republican victory. 
In 1939, Austria's <span style="color: #7A3C00;">Austrofascist</span> regime collapses under popular revolt.
In 1938, Austria's ruling <span style="color: #A50034;">
SDAPÃ
</span> successfully holds a referendum confirming their <i>Anschluss</i> into Germany. 
The war in Austria is eventually won by the <span style="color: #7A3C00;">Austrofascists</span>, who receive generous aid from a <span style="color: #7A3C00;">fascist</span> Italy.
 Isolated and denied any success in his expansionist aims in Yugoslavia and Greece, Mussolini is deposed in a royalist coup led by King Victor Emmanuel III, and Italy returns to democratic governance
, leading to Austriaâs government democratizing as a consequence of losing its benefactor
..
Africa remains colonized until the 1970s and 80s. Independence, when it comes, is negotiated largely on the terms of the colonizers. Exploitation persists under a different guise. The Cold War unfolds along different lines, as a "state capitalist" <span style="color: #750e0e;">Soviet Union</span> remains intact, forming a bloc with the authoritarian <span style="color: #000099;">Kuomintang</span>-ruled Republic of China and post-independence India, which fought a bloody war to escape <span style="color: #C8102E;">British</span> rule in the 1950s. This tri-polar alliance has solidified into a global axis of its own.

"MEFO Bills"
Ordered by Hjalmar Schacht of the Economic Ministry.

Subending A - A False Victory
The fracturing of the <span style="color: #c00000;">
SPD
</span> into the <span style="color: #f0456a;">
SVD
</span> and the <span style="color: #9B0000;">

</span> in the wake of post-war optimism deeply divided the German left. As in 1920, leftist disunity once again produced a scenario where a "New <span style="color: #000000;">We</span><span style="color: #DD0000;">im</span><span style="color: #FFCC00;">ar</span> Coalition"âcomposed of the <span style="color: #f0456a;">

</span>, <span style="color: #FFCC00;">LVP</span>, and <span style="color: #FF7F00;">
CSRP
</span>âwas unable to secure a majority. This led to repeated snap elections, ultimately fueling another resurgence of extremism.
It was only through the formation of the <span style="color: #BFC8CC;">German National Unity Front</span>âa broad alliance of all bourgeois partiesâand the election of Otto Gessler as president that Germany finally escaped its political deadlock. The resulting coalition of the <span style="color: #000000;">
ZCA
</span>, <span style="color: #FFCC00;">
LVP
</span>, and <span style="color: #38C538;">
DBP
</span> emerged as the new ânatural governing bloc,â as persistent ideological rifts continued to paralyze the left. The <span style="color: #f0456a;">
SVD
</span>, increasingly marginalized, was at times compelled to tolerate these centrist cabinets in the name of stabilityâabandoning its <span style="color: #9B0000;">Marxist</span> roots in the process and ceding leadership of the left to the more radical <span style="color: #9B0000;">
SED
</span>.
Only with the third wave of democratization in the 1990s did the long-divided German left begin to reunify. The gradual moderation and eventual merger of the <span style="color: #9B0000;">
SED
</span> and the <span style="color: #f0456a;">
SVD
</span> culminated in the formation of the <span style="color: #c00000;">
VSPD
</span>, which at last returned to government after decades in opposition.
<span style="color: #BFC8CC;">
DNEF
</span> - <i>Deutschnationale Einheitsfront</i> (German National Unity Front) - A "presidential" party founded by Chancellor Kurt von Schleicher and his allies, this movement embraces a syncretic ideology that defies easy categorization within the political spectrum. At its core, it is built on militarism, nationalism, and corporatism. The party garners support from urbanites, blue-collar workers, the middle classes, and even some of the unemployed, all united by their shared loyalty to the chancellor.

Paul Lejeune-Jung, leader of the <span style="color: #3E88B3;">
DNVP
</span>'s Catholic wing, and an advocate for closer ties to the <span style="color: #000000;">Center</span> has been elected as leader of a merger between the two. His Catholic faith has made him acceptable to former <span style="color: #000000;">Center</span> members, and his alignment with Westarp's faction has solidified <span style="color: #3E88B3;">nationalist</span> support.
However, Lejeune-Jung's role as a compromise candidate has hindered his ability to effect significant change, as he must tread carefully to avoid alienating the factions that helped bring him to power. Lejeune-Jung will attempt to bridge the confessional divides between Catholic and Protestant conservatives, with the party still divided along the lines of their party of <span class="tooltip-text" title="that's me! the modman!">origin</span>.
It goes without saying, this will make cooperation within the party impossible for the foreseeable future.

This yearâs <span style="color: #D3C24D;">

Constitutional Reform
Constitutional Reform
Many neorevisionists consider the <span style="color: #000000;">We</span><span style="color: #DD0000;">im</span><span style="color: #FFCC00;">ar</span> Constitution to be inadequate. They believe that the clauses of the constitution are in part responsible for the instability of the country, and must change in order to build a more stable democratic republic. Ideas from both <span style="color: #c00000;">socialists</span> and <span style="color: #D3C24D;">liberals</span>.
A constitutional reform requires either a two-thirds supermajority in the Reichstag, or a constitutional referendum with support from a majority of the electorate. If we do not have a supermajority in the Reichstag, we must be assured of strong support from the other parties for the constitutional referendum.

German Labor Front
Schleicher has been working intensively over the past weeks to forge a formal alliance between the trade unions and the state, focusing his efforts on the <span style="color: #000000;">Christian</span> associations. Their negotiations have now "succeeded"âwith heavy doses of state intimidation and interference ensuring they accepted an offer that would have been unthinkable under normal circumstances.
Under this agreement, the blue-collar <span style="color: #D3C24D;">Hirsch-Duncker</span>, <span style="color: #000000;">Christian</span>, and the Strasserist <span style="color: #7A3C00;">
NSBO
</span> unions are merged into the new German Labor Front (DAF). White-collar and civil servant unions, including the DHV, are folded into their own section within the Front. The right to strike is curtailed, and all members are made to pledge full loyalty to the so-called "national interest"âhowever the state chooses to define it.
Local union leaders who refuse to join are having their offices raided, their funds confiscated, and themselves imprisoned. While membership in the German Labor Front is technically voluntary, access to benefitsâand even basic protectionsâis now locked behind it, making participation all but mandatory. Furthermore, a new department is established in the DAF, tasked solely with spreading state propaganda and binding the workers more tightly into the <i>Volksgemeinschaft</i>.
The chancellor is also able to palate the industrialists, by placing their power significantly above that of labor in the newly established corporatist framework.

Addressing the Economic Crisis
Our party is wracked as to how to deal with the economic crisis. From the centrist faction, Hilferding argues that the economic crisis must be allowed to play out, because it is simply a part of the cyclic nature of capitalism.
The Labor faction is arguing for increased welfare spending to alleviate the suffering of the workers. Their economist, Wladimir Woytinsky, has voiced support for a public works campaign involving deficit spending, but it will take time to iron out the details. Furthermore, he will need the united support of the ADGB; otherwise, he won't have the power to challenge Hilferding's stance. 
From labor, the economist Wladimir Woytinsky argues that we must pursue a massive public works program to reduce unemployment and stimulate demand, perhaps including deficit spending. His opponents, like Hilferding, argue that this plan will cause a return to hyper-inflation, and that it would not even help the economy. In addition, according to Hilferding, Woytinsky's strategy is opposed to Marxist principles. 
The so-called WTB plan, after its chief supporters Woytinsky, Tarnow, and Baade, is receiving more attention within the party. 
However, leftists in the party argue that the WTB plan is merely a bandage over the fundamental flaws of capitalism. They argue that the crisis is an opportunity to end capitalism through massive nationalizations of industry, and finally bring our country to <span style="color: #c00000;">socialism</span>.
Meanwhile, moderates and reformists draw up proposals for a limited plan of job creation through public works, funded through taxes, that does not involve deficit spending or large-scale restructuring of the economy. Labor argues that the plan is inadequate in its scale and ambition.
Together with the <span style="color: #000000;">Center</span>, we share a common opposition to the incumbent cabinet, making improving relations easier.
Expel the centrists.
The <span style="color: #c00000;">Social Democrats</span> have been and will always be a party of the working class.

Building a People's Party
Contrary to orthodox Marxist predictions, the industrial proletariat is not constantly increasing in size. The rapid growth of the "new middle class" of white-collar workers and the persistence of small farmers and the peasantry confound predictions of a working-class majority.
In order to build a <span style="color: #c00000;">socialist</span> majority, many reformists and neorevisionists are calling for our party to move beyond the urban working class, to embrace the concerns of the middle class, farmers, and women of all classes. We can create a new and inclusive vision of the party that encompasses all people who work for a living, which is almost everyone in the country save the aristocrats and top capitalists.

</span> control over the Rhineland. The fragile alliance of <span style="color: #7A3C00;">far</span>-<span style="color: #3E88B3;">right</span> forces soon fractures, with the <span style="color: #3E88B3;">
Stahlhelm
</span> and the <span style="color: #7A3C00;">
SA
</span> turning against one another in violent internal conflict.
Ernst RÃ¶hm eventually reduces 
 to a puppet, seizing full control and unleashing unspeakable atrocities against the local <span style="color: #005EB8;">Jewish</span> and Polish populations in the territories under their control. A renewed offensive by <span style="color: #000000;">rep</span><span style="color: #DD0000;">ubl</span><span style="color: #FFCC00;">ican</span> forces exploits the chaos on the Right, culminating in the defeat of the reactionary militias. However, a brutal campaign continues in the Ruhr, where every town exacts a death toll of thousands.
By 1936, Germany remains fractured. Poland has seized the moment to occupy parts of East Prussia and Silesia, citing the protection of ethnic Poles. Meanwhile, the <span style="color: #002654;">French</span> dispatch a "peacekeeping" force into the Rhineland. In the end, it is not so much a victory for the Republic as it is a decisive collapse of the <span style="color: #7A3C00;">far</span>-<span style="color: #3E88B3;">right</span>.
No war as destructive as the Great War would come again. Slowly, Germany and the <span style="color: #0A3161;">Western</span> <span style="color: #C8102E;">Allies</span> began to rebuild relations, but the <span style="color: #002654;">French</span> reoccupation of the Rhineland has complicated efforts. <span style="color: #7A3C00;">Fascism</span> never takes root as a dominant force, and the primary threat to European democracy becomes the resurgent <span style="color: #750e0e;">Soviet Union</span>. The Spanish Civil War ends in 1936 with a Republican victory. 
In 1939, Austria's <span style="color: #7A3C00;">Austrofascist</span> regime collapses under popular revolt.
In 1938, Austria's ruling <span style="color: #A50034;">
SDAPÃ
</span> successfully holds a referendum confirming their <i>Anschluss</i> into Germany. 
The war in Austria is eventually won by the <span style="color: #7A3C00;">Austrofascists</span>, who receive generous aid from a <span style="color: #7A3C00;">fascist</span> Italy.
 Isolated and denied any success in his expansionist aims in Yugoslavia and Greece, Mussolini is deposed in a royalist coup led by King Victor Emmanuel III, and Italy returns to democratic governance
, leading to Austriaâs government democratizing as a consequence of losing its benefactor
.
Africa remains colonized until the 1970s and 80s. Independence, when it comes, is negotiated largely on the terms of the colonizers. Exploitation persists under a different guise. The Cold War unfolds along different lines, as a "state capitalist" <span style="color: #750e0e;">Soviet Union</span> remains intact, forming a bloc with the authoritarian <span style="color: #000099;">Kuomintang</span>-ruled Republic of China and post-independence India, which fought a bloody war to escape <span style="color: #C8102E;">British</span> rule in the 1950s. This tri-polar alliance has solidified into a global axis of its own.
Internal factions of the
 <span style="color: #c00000;">
SPD
</span>
Overall dissent: 
Left
Many leftists are former members of the <span style="color: #D47B9B;">
USPD
</span> (the left-wing, anti-war <span style="color: #D47B9B;">Independent Social Democratic Party</span>), or are young radicals. Many are interested in closer cooperation with the <span style="color: #700000;">
KPD
</span>, and some support extra-parliamentary action.
Strength: 
Dissent: 
Centrists
Centrists in the <span style="color: #c00000;">
SPD
</span> are followers of the doctrine of orthodox <span style="color: #9B0000;">Marxism</span> as expounded by Karl Kautsky and later Rudolf Hilferding. They seek a middle course between reform and revolution, while defending the democratic state.
Strength: 
Dissent: 
Labor
Many <span style="color: #c00000;">
SPD
</span> members are also part of the ADGB, the federation of free trade unions. Their goal is economic reform and benefits for organized workers.
Strength: 
Dissent: 
Reformists
Reformists are pragmatic participants in government, and seek to expand the welfare state and defend democracy against the <span style="color: #7A3C00;">far</span>-<span style="color: #3E88B3;">right</span> and <span style="color: #700000;">left</span>. 
They are dominant in the state government of Prussia.

Increase educational opportunities for the working class by funding public high schools.
-1 budget
We have funded more public secondary schools so that proletarian children may receive the same education as middle and upper-class children, and can be prepared for universities.
Raise tariffs on imports.
+2 budget. Other countries will retaliate by increasing tariffs of their own. 
Further increases in tariffs will not raise revenue, as we have already nearly reached economic autarky.
Increasing tariffs leads to higher prices for basic goods, increasing inflation and hurting consumption among the working class. Retaliatory tariffs reduce the competitiveness of German exports, raising unemployment among workers. They also lead to an atmosphere of international suspicion, reducing our ability to negotiate with other countries.
On the other hand, agricultural producers and some small businesses benefit from higher tariffs, as farmers can now command higher prices for grain, and formerly uncompetitive businesses are now able to function.
Form a customs union with Austria?
This will likely incur the wrath of <span style="color: #002654;">France</span>.
This is superseded by the <span style="color: #001489;">European</span> <span style="color: #FFDD00;">Union</span>.
Yes, do it!
No, we should do something else.
The right has long desired closer ties with Austria, with the eventual goal of the merger of the two German-speaking countries. A customs union with Austria is the first step towards this goal. Additionally, many believe that a union with Austria would improve our current economic circumstances.
However, the Allies, especially <span style="color: #002654;">France</span>, are likely to object. This agreement would break the terms of the Versailles Treaty, and could lead to economic sanctions, further hurting our economy.

Economic Policy
The debate over agriculture, welfare, and the depression.
A potential big-tent <span style="color: #000000;">Christian People's Party</span> faces the difficult task of addressing the diverse economic concerns of its broad electoral base. This base spans across farmers, aristocrats, landowners, large businesses, small businesses, civil servants, white-collar workers, blue-collar urban workers, and many others from all walks of life.

A potential big-tent <span style="color: #000000;">Christian People's Party</span> faces the difficult task of addressing the diverse economic concerns of its broad electoral base. This base spans across farmers, aristocrats, landowners, large businesses, small businesses, civil servants, white-collar workers, blue-collar urban workers, and many others from all walks of life.
Kuno von Westarp, formerly of the <span style="color: #3E88B3;">DNVP</span>, is elected as their new leader.
A long-time <span style="color: #3E88B3;">DNVP</span> stalwart, having prevented Hugenberg's takeover of the party in the past. - <span style="color: #3E88B3;">National Conservative</span> Wing
Westarp is reluctant, and the party needs a new face. - <span style="color: #3E88B3;">National Conservative</span> Wing
A key figure of the German Right since the Republic's inception, Kuno von Westarp has ascended to the leadership role of the colossal <span style="color: #000000;">Christian People's Party</span>âcertainly a significant step up. His election was primarily driven by the unified support of the entire former <span style="color: #3E88B3;">nationalist</span> faction within the party.
Westarp is highly experienced in managing internal dissent and is careful not to lean too heavily into any one particular wing of the party, aiming to avoid fracturing the organization. While the party program remains largely unchanged, there has been a noticeable increase in antisemitic and pro-monarchist rhetoric, as Westarp seeks to preserve the character of the now-defunct <span style="color: #3E88B3;">
DNVP
</span> within this new entity.
It goes without saying, Westarp's leadership will make cooperation within the party impossible for the foreseeable future.

His election was primarily driven by the unified support of the entire former <span style="color: #3E88B3;">nationalist</span> faction within the party.
Westarp is highly experienced in managing internal dissent and is careful not to lean too heavily into any one particular wing of the party, aiming to avoid fracturing the organization. While the party program remains largely unchanged, there has been a noticeable increase in antisemitic and pro-monarchist rhetoric, as Westarp seeks to preserve the character of the now-defunct <span style="color: #3E88B3;">

It goes without saying, Westarp's leadership will make cooperation within the party impossible for the foreseeable future.

Subending A - The Black Spot of Europe
Despite the Republicâs paper victory, revanchist sentiment over the loss of Eastern and Western territories, coupled with mass radicalization from the staggering death toll, destruction, hyperinflation, widespread hunger, and the absence of meaningful foreign aid, has deeply discredited the political establishment. Under pressure from the Reichswehr, martial law remains in place for years, with the military increasingly intruding into civilian governance.
By 1939, a global recessionâtriggered by escalating instability across Europe and threats from the <span style="color: #750e0e;">Soviet</span> Unionâobliterates the reconstruction progress made by the ruling <span style="color: #c00000;">
SPD
</span>, plunging the country into yet another crisis. Following a March on Berlin, a "Third Positionist" regime seizes power, led by a new political party composed of former <span style="color: #7A3C00;">Nazis</span> and disillusioned <span style="color: #c00000;">socialists</span>.
Germany pivots toward autarky and radical self-reliance, sealing its borders to foreigners. The regime earns the moniker "Black Spot of Europe," ruling from 1940 to 1996. During this period, it successfully coerces the withdrawal of foreign troops from the Rhineland and reclaims its lost eastern territoriesâincluding the Polish Corridorâin 1946, capitalizing on Poland's distraction with the <span style="color: #750e0e;">Soviet</span> threat and the shifting priorities of the <span style="color: #0A3161;">Western</span> <span style="color: #C8102E;">Allies</span> as they fight war with Japan.
Today, Germany remains largely isolated from the rest of the world, with the successor to the dictatorship of old being not much better, being ruled by oligarchs and the wealthy who are relicts of the past regime. Now, the task of <span style="color: #c00000;">Social Democracy</span> is to prepare for the next election. The incumbent government of Wolfgang SchÃ¤uble is unpopular, and we stand to gain. Perhaps the next government will have a <span style="color: #c00000;">socialist</span> plurality.

Create elected school boards to give local areas more control over education.
We have created local elected school boards, and delegated some of the decision-making for local schools to them.
Lower tariffs on imports.
-2 budget. 
There are no tariffs left to cut.
Lowering tariffs helps workers and the urban poor by reducing prices for basic necessities. In addition, other countries are pleased by lower tariffs, and are more willing to negotiate on many issues.
Farmers are hurt by this policy, as cheap imported grain leads to ruin for some small producers. In general, cheap imports are detrimental for small-scale industries aimed at the domestic market. Those negatively affected have rallied behind the

Fiscal Policy
Fiscal Policy
Fiscal policy involves issues of taxation and budget.
We have formed a customs union with Austria! The right-wing parties are praising us for this unexpected turn of events.
Immediately, <span style="color: #002654;">France</span> and other neighboring countries lodge their protests. The longer-term response is yet to be seen.
We have signed a Concordat between the Free State of Prussia and the Roman Catholic Church, guaranteeing religious freedom for Catholics in Prussia. This sets the stage for the negotiation of a national Concordat covering all of Germany.

Prussian Affairs
Prussia, by far the largest and most important state in Germany, is governed solely by our party, free from the compromises demanded by the bourgeois parties. This grants us the opportunity to pursue meaningful reforms. Under the capable leadership of Otto Braun, Prussia stands as the most stable and democratic government in the Republic.
All our actions have increased effectiveness.
We can enact policies in Prussia.
Despite the defections of some members of the <span style="color: #3E88B3;">
DNVP
</span>'s parliamentary delegation to the <span style="color: #003755;">
DNF
</span>, BrÃ¼ning's government has retained the support of 
% of the Reichstag. However, despite this victory, the episode has severely radicalized the <span style="color: #3E88B3;">
DNVP
</span>'s voters, leading many to defect to the more radical <span style="color: #003755;">
DNF
</span> and even the <span style="color: #7A3C00;">
NSDAP
</span>.
We don't have that power anymore. You really screwed us here with your reforms, you know that?
We have sacked BrÃ¼ning and called for new elections for 
.
Our relations are not strong enough.
We send a call for help to the West, but their governments are divided on whether or not to support us. We have to survive on our own until they make a decision.

War Hero Paul von Lettow-Vorbeck, formerly of the <span style="color: #3E88B3;">DNVP</span>, is elected as their new leader.
Lettow-Vorbeck is reluctant to lead, but perhaps his war hero status can serve as a unifying force? - <span style="color: #3E88B3;">National Conservative</span> Wing
Lettow-Vorbeck is reluctant to lead, and prefers his unglamorous life in the Reichstag. Unless he is convinced, of course. - <span style="color: #3E88B3;">National Conservative</span> Wing
Paul von Lettow-Vorbeck is a war hero renowned for his undefeated record and his successful guerrilla campaign in German East Africa, with victories that rival those of Hindenburg. Despite multiple attempts to involve him in German politics, he has remained largely inactive, serving as a member of the Reichstag elected under the <span style="color: #3E88B3;">
DNVP
</span>. However, the 
unending deadlock at the congress
drastic decline in the moderate right's vote share
desire to utilize his character for a potential future election run
 has led to a growing number of factions rallying around his candidacy. The widespread popular support for him draws strong parallels to Hindenburg's own presidential candidacy declaration in 1925.
Reluctantly, Lettow-Vorbeck has accepted the <span style="color: #000000;">
</span>'s nomination as their next leader. However, he has little interest in managing the partyâs internal affairs and has quietly delegated this responsibility to fellow conservatives like Westarp. Nonetheless, he remains the public face of the <span style="color: #000000;">
</span> as chairman. The most crucial aspect of his election is his ability to inspire the German people to rally behind a Great War war hero once again, someone who embodies the image of stability and unity. Time will tell how long this popularity will last.
It goes without saying, his election will make cooperation within the party impossible for the foreseeable future.
First <span style="color: #000000;">
</span> Congress
The future of this new party will be decided here.
First <span style="color: #000000;">
</span> Congress
The <span style="color: #000000;">Christian People's Party</span> (<span style="color: #000000;">
</span>) has officially been founded! Adam Stegerwald, orchestrator of this arrangement and leader of the former <span style="color: #000000;">Center</span>, has been chosen as the interim leader of the new party.
The <span style="color: #000000;">
</span>'s inaugural congress will play a major role in shaping the direction of the newly founded party, and to elect a new chairman. It finally looks like we might have some competition now.
War Hero Paul von Lettow-Vorbeck, formerly of the <span style="color: #3E88B3;">DNVP</span>, is elected as their new leader.
Lettow-Vorbeck is reluctant to lead, but perhaps his war hero status can serve as a unifying force? - <span style="color: #3E88B3;">National Conservative</span> Wing

Paul von Lettow-Vorbeck is a war hero renowned for his undefeated record and his successful guerrilla campaign in German East Africa, with victories that rival those of Hindenburg. Despite multiple attempts to involve him in German politics, he has remained largely inactive, serving as a member of the Reichstag elected under the <span style="color: #3E88B3;">
DNVP
</span>. However, the 
unending deadlock at the congress
drastic decline in the moderate right's vote share
desire to utilize his character for a potential future election run
 has led to a growing number of factions rallying around his candidacy. The widespread popular support for him draws strong parallels to Hindenburg's own presidential candidacy declaration in 1925.
Reluctantly, Lettow-Vorbeck has accepted the <span style="color: #000000;">
</span>'s nomination as their next leader. However, he has little interest in managing the partyâs internal affairs and has quietly delegated this responsibility to fellow conservatives like Westarp. Nonetheless, he remains the public face of the <span style="color: #000000;">
</span> as chairman. The most crucial aspect of his election is his ability to inspire the German people to rally behind a Great War war hero once again, someone who embodies the image of stability and unity. Time will tell how long this popularity will last.
It goes without saying, his election will make cooperation within the party impossible for the foreseeable future.

 will be resigning his positions to accept the presidency.
Second <span style="color: #000000;">
</span> Congress
With the election of 
âthe party chairmanâas president, he naturally cannot retain both roles. He has since resigned, prompting a new party congress to select his successor. The partyâs various factions are eager to assert themselves and steer its future direction. The outgoing chairman, now president, continues to wield considerable influence and is expected to endorse a candidate from his own faction.
The <span style="color: #3E88B3;">nationalists</span> and <span style="color: #385A38;">agrarians</span> have used their leverage to edit in their economic beliefs into the party program.
Taxation - Avoid tax increases at all costs, especially on businesses
Welfare - Germany cannot afford welfare and maintain a balanced budget at the same time, one has to go
Labor - Strongly against any <span style="color: #c00000;">socialistic</span> policies that benefit the unions
Great Depression - Maintain fiscal orthodoxy, productive industries and agriculture must be kept afloat though
The next president should be a symbol of the new Germany...
Albert Einstein, physicist.
Heinrich Mann, left-wing writer.
Carl von Ossietzky, pacifist journalist.
Actually, we should nominate someone else.
Since the presidency has been made a largely ceremonial figure by our reforms, we might set a precedent by nominating a nonpartisan pro-democracy figure from the cultural, scientific, or literary worlds. There are many potential candidates...
Let the voting begin.

</span> agitators of arson. As the accused await trial, President Hindenburg and the cabinet insist the fire was meant to signal the start of a <span style="color: #700000;">
Communist

Subending B - A <span style="color: #001489;">European</span> <span style="color: #FFDD00;">Future</span>
In exchange for the return of the Rhineland and the lifting of its post-war reparations burden, Germany is compelled to enter a defensive pact with <span style="color: #002654;">France</span> and <span style="color: #C8102E;">Britain</span>. With assistance from the <span style="color: #10069f;">League</span> of <span style="color: #10069f;">Nations</span> and the <span style="color: #B31942;">United</span> <span style="color: #0A3161;">States</span>, the so-called "Morgenthau Plan", funded by the <span style="color: #0A3161;">Western</span> <span style="color: #C8102E;">Allies</span>, successfully facilitates German reconstruction and ushers in a <i>Wirtschaftswunder</i> under the leadership of Chancellor Konrad Adenauer.
By the end of the decade, the economies of the <span style="color: #0A3161;">West</span> become increasingly interconnected. Further integration into a nascent "<span style="color: #001489;">European</span> <span style="color: #FFDD00;">Union</span>"âfeaturing free trade and the free movement of peopleâis negotiated by the internationalist <span style="color: #c00000;">
SPD
</span> and the <span style="color: #FFCC00;">
LVP
</span>. Renewed <span style="color: #750e0e;">Soviet</span> threats to Eastern European nations like Finland and Poland, alongside the democratization of Austria and Italy, have once again galvanized a united European response to <span style="color: #750e0e;">Bolshevik</span> aggression.
Today, Germany remains a pillar of a federal <span style="color: #001489;">European</span> <span style="color: #FFDD00;">Union</span> stretching from Lisbon to Athens. Now, the task of <span style="color: #f0456a;">Social Democracy</span> is to prepare for the next election. The incumbent government of Klaus Schwab is unpopular, and we stand to gain. Perhaps the next government will have a <span style="color: #f0456a;">socialist</span> plurality.

Many <span style="color: #c00000;">socialists</span> believe that education in Germany is not fit for the modern era. Much of the educational system still follows imperial-era traditions of militaristic discipline, and <span style="color: #7A3C00;">far</span>-<span style="color: #3E88B3;">right</span> professors and students rule the universities. Nevertheless, there have been strides in bringing working-class children into the schools.

The <span class="tooltip-text" title="Following Hindenburg's overseeing of Germany's crushing victory at Tannenberg in the Great War, Hindenburg has become the center of a massive personality cult, lauded as the living ideal of German masculinity and patriotism. Return of the Kaiserreich
Ending 6
With the <span style="color: #7A3C00;">Nazi</span> movement in decline following the end of the economic depression and an internal leadership struggle, and with the election of the <span style="color: #3E88B3;">nationalist</span> Hergt alongside the appointment of the <span style="color: #3E88B3;">
Stahlhelm
</span>'s Seldte, it appears that <span style="color: #808080;">reaction</span> has triumphed over <span style="color: #7A3C00;">revolution</span>. Although various neo-<span style="color: #7A3C00;">Nazi</span> splinters emerged after 
âs final descent into delusion, the ideology itself remains irrevocably discredited.
Following the peaceful return of the Saarland via referendum in 1935, the Reichswehr suppressed an attempted uprising by the <span style="color: #7A3C00;">
SA
</span> and purged the remaining <span style="color: #7A3C00;">Nazi</span> elements from government. In the heavily managed 1936 elections, under the watch of "electoral monitors", the <span style="color: #808080;">monarchist</span> parties secured a manufactured mandate. Soon after, a secret rearmament program was launched, and shortly after, the crowning of Wilhelm IIIâblessed by his fatherârestores Germany's constitutional monarchy.
With ambitions focused on reclaiming lost territories in the East and Alsace-Lorraine in the West, Germany began courting allies for its revisionist aims. The restoration of Habsburg rule in Austria and the toppling of Mussolini in a royalist coup led by King Victor Emmanuel III drastically altered the balance of power in Europe. When Poland refused to return former imperial lands, the <span style="color: #C8102E;">British</span>, and more reluctantly the <span style="color: #002654;">French</span>, turned a blind eye to escalating German border skirmishes. The <span style="color: #700000;">Communist</span> victory in Spain, and the wave of revolutions it triggered across the continent, forced the imperial powers to prioritize containment of the <span style="color: #700000;">Soviet</span> threat above all else.
Africa remains colonized until the 1980s and 90s. Independence, when it comes, is negotiated largely on the terms of the colonizers. Exploitation persists under a different guise. The Cold War unfolds along different lines, as a "state capitalist" <span style="color: #750e0e;">Soviet Union</span> remains intact, forming a bloc with the authoritarian <span style="color: #000099;">Kuomintang</span>-ruled Republic of China and post-independence India, which fought a bloody war to escape <span style="color: #C8102E;">British</span> rule in the 1950s. This tri-polar alliance evolved into a global axis of its own, encompassing numerous <span style="color: #700000;">Communist</span> regimes worldwide.
Today, Germany is a dominant party state, ruled by the elites of the <span style="color: #3E88B3;">
DNVP
</span>, who have maintained control for nearly a century. Although dissatisfaction with the monarchy and the growing strength of the opposition have forced some minor reforms, each new election consistently delivers a majority for the incumbent coalition. However, cracks are beginning to show in the system. New, young, and popular <span style="color: #c00000;">
SPD
</span> leaders are making inroads despite state repression, with Sahra Wagenknecht being the first to achieve a significant victory, having been elected Minister-President of Thuringia. Perhaps the next government will have a mere <span style="color: #3E88B3;">nationalist</span> plurality.

Unemployment will start at 100%.
We have begun purchasing idling enterprises from the capitalists
, and running them in coordination with empowered works councils 
. By keeping production going, we can stave off the rising unemployment. 
The Left argues that this should only be a first step in the <span style="color: #c00000;">socialist</span> transformation of the economy. Eventually, much of the economy must be brought under popular control. 
We purchase additional underutilized enterprises and run them in a coordinated <span style="color: #c00000;">socialist</span> fashion with the works councils, hiring formerly laid off workers. A significant portion of the economy is now under public control.
Democratic curriculum
Critical thinking, democratic citizenship, and collaboration
We should teach students how to be citizens of a democratic republic, by teaching them to critically evaluate politics and current events, to exercise their rights and to respect the rights of other citizens. Traditionalists of all stripes are critical of this curriculum.

The Imperfect Dictatorship
Ending 7
Despite being hated by nearly 90% of the population, Franz von Papen's surprising victoryâsecured through the backing of the elites and the Reichswehrâbrought him to power in a deeply fractured Germany. However, consolidating that power proved difficult. Despite the eradication of organized <span style="color: #700000;">communist</span> and <span style="color: #c00000;">social democratic</span> resistance, the Ruhr remains ablaze with unrest. Meanwhile, ongoing conflict between Papen and Kurt von Schleicher, who still maintains significant influence within the military, further destabilizes his regime.
Papen's nationalist foreign policy has won him few friends abroad, and his goal of unshackling Germany from the chains of Versailles remains distant. In an effort to restore order, Papen forms a "National Regeneration Government", rooted in conservative Christian values, militarism, and monarchism. Following the death of President Paul von Hindenburg, Wilhelm III is crowned as a figurehead monarch, while real power remains concentrated in the hands of the political elite and industrial magnates. Germany is transformed into a dictatorship, with the "New State" envisioned by Papen centralizing authority in the executive.
After a period of economic recovery in the late 1930s, Papen turns his attention toward foreign policy, seeking to broker an entente with the <span style="color: #002654;">French</span> to combat <span style="color: #700000;">Bolshevism</span> in the East. When these efforts failed, he turned to cooperate with <span style="color: #7A3C00;">Austrofascist</span> Austria and Mussoliniâs Italy, who remained among Germanyâs few continental allies.
However by the 1950s, however, the regime begins to decay. Corruption, unrest, and economic stagnationâcoupled with Papenâs deathâtrigger a political thaw. The "Prussian Spring" ushers in a wave of democratic reforms, but the entrenched elites of the old system work to protect their interests. The military retains an active and unofficial role in politics, making it impossible for the reconstituted <span style="color: #c00000;">
SPD
</span> to assume power without risking a coup.
Africa remains colonized until the 1980s and 90s. Independence, when it comes, is negotiated largely on the terms of the colonizers. Exploitation persists under a different guise. The Cold War unfolds along different lines, as a <span style="color: #750e0e;">Soviet Union</span> surviving intact but slightly liberalized, serving as the key pillar in a global alliance encompassing the <span style="color: #700000;">Communist</span> regimes worldwide.
Today, Germany is a multiparty democracy, following the ousting of the successor conservative party founded by figures of the former regime. Yet all is not well in the republic. Friedrich Merz, now President, has drawn criticism for invoking nostalgia for the old system, sparking fears of democratic backsliding and concentration of power in favor of the oligarchs. Now, the task of <span style="color: #f0456a;">Social Democracy</span> is to prepare for the next presidential election. Our Sahra Wagenknecht is considering running for another non-consecutive term; perhaps she is our only hope to topple Merz.

Taking any of these actions would require a vast departure from our previous party stances.

Our educational system should reflect the modern age, and we should teach science and rational thinking. Religious conservatives tend to react negatively against this.

Additionally, <span style="color: #002654;">France</span>'s new <span style="color: #E0B0FF;">left-wing</span> Prime Minister has been considerably more amicable than their predecessor. As a result, we have secured their agreement to a conference aimed at finally settling the reparations issue.
In the end, Germany is required to make only a final payment of three billion marks, effectively ending future reparation obligations. This has greatly boosted investor confidence and sparked a new wave of capital inflows to Germany. The United Front Government, under President

In 1937, after winning a second election and slightly increasing its majority, the government faced a coup attempt led by the <span style="color: #808080;">reactionary</span> elements of the Reichswehr and increasingly radicalized <span style="color: #3E88B3;">nationalists</span> and <span style="color: #7A3C00;">far</span>-<span style="color: #3E88B3;">right</span> forces.
The coup failed. The abortive putsch was crushed by the proletarian masses and shattered the political landscape of <span style="color: #000000;">We</span><span style="color: #DD0000;">im</span><span style="color: #FFCC00;">ar</span>. The far left of the 
<span style="color: #D3C24D;">

</span>

<span style="color: #000000;">Center</span>

âsplinters from their parties who maintained ambiguity towards the coupâsplit off to form the <span style="color: #E0B0FF;">
RDP
</span> and <span style="color: #FF7F00;">
CSRP
</span>, both of which soon joined the United Front.
With the bourgeois center increasingly marginalized, the botched coup gave the United Front the momentum needed to finally purge the Republic of <span style="color: #808080;">reactionary</span> forces and initiate a <span style="color: #9B0000;">socialist</span> economy. Eventually a constitutional convention was called to begin a <span style="color: #9B0000;">Socialist Republic</span>.

Root Scene
Take a budget deficit to fund the nationalization program.
Woytinsky can bring us around to supporting a budget deficit.
The party does not yet support budget deficits for this purpose. 
Perhaps bringing on an advisor might help make this action palatable?
Most of the party, even on the left, are opposed to budget deficits for any purpose, with the exception of Wladimir Woytinsky. While he did not originally support the left's "transformational" plan, he has come around, and has persuaded the party and the Finance Ministry to support budget deficits in limited cases, for the purpose of exiting from the depression. 
We have begun purchasing idling enterprises from the capitalists
, and running them in coordination with empowered works councils 
. By keeping production going, we can stave off the rising unemployment. 
The Left argues that this should only be a first step in the <span style="color: #c00000;">socialist</span> transformation of the economy. Eventually, much of the economy must be brought under popular control. 
We purchase additional underutilized enterprises and run them in a coordinated <span style="color: #c00000;">socialist</span> fashion with the works councils, hiring formerly laid off workers. A significant portion of the economy is now under public control.
Humanist curriculum
Arts, literature, history, and philosophy
Our educational curriculum should be focused on the humanities, including arts, literature, history, and philosophy. Some criticize this curriculum as focusing too much on "useless" subjects, while others worry that the arts establishment is too closely tied to romantic nationalism.
But of course, Schleicher stipulates that we demand military parity.
This will be a severe betrayal of our values!
General Schleicher has demanded that we raise the issue of rearmament, which remains restricted under the Treaty of Versailles. However, <span style="color: #002654;">French</span> Premier Ãdouard Herriot seized on this as an admission that Germany is, in fact, capable of paying its reparations and would simply redirect freed-up funds toward rearmament.
However, with the formation of the <span style="color: #001489;">European</span> <span style="color: #FFDD00;">Union</span> and its defense agreements, preventing Germanyâs rearmament has become increasingly irrelevant. As a result, military restrictions are lifted!
However, our exceptionally friendly relations with the <span style="color: #0A3161;">Western</span> <span style="color: #C8102E;">Allies</span> have led them to begrudgingly grant us flexibility in rearmament, believing that we could serve as a bulwark against the <span style="color: #700000;">Bolsheviks</span>.

NSDAP
</span> has lost yet another propaganda tool. However, we continue to suffer heavy losses to the <span style="color: #700000;">

&nbsp;and <span style="color: #D3C24D;">

This has caused great embarrassment to our pacifist and democratic image, both at home and abroad. Damn you, Schleicher!

Nationalize without compensation.
There is no reason why we should not simply take over idle factories. This is likely to provoke a confrontation with the capitalists.

We have begun acquiring idling enterprises from the capitalists
, and running them in coordination with empowered works councils 
. By keeping production going, we can stave off the rising unemployment.

Alongside the <span style="color: #750e0e;">Soviet</span> Bloc, the <span style="color: #750e0e;">Soviet Republic</span> of Germany largely wins the Cold War. We can sign a treaty to form a <span style="color: #001489;">European</span> <span style="color: #FFDD00;">Union</span>!
Along with <span style="color: #002654;">France</span>, <span style="color: #C8102E;">Great Britain</span>, and many other states of Western and Eastern Europe, we have signed a treaty to form a <span style="color: #001489;">European</span> <span style="color: #FFDD00;">Union</span>! It is hoped that <span style="color: #001489;">Europe</span> has learned the lessons of the Great War; never again will there be another war that tears the continent apart.
A major component of the union is the free movement of people and goods across the countries of Europe, as well as the stabilization of currency exchange rates. This provides new markets for German products and new sources of investment.
<span style="color: #7A3C00;">Fascist</span> Italy remains a problem, and the <span style="color: #750e0e;">Soviet Union</span> also lies outside the system. But for now, there is new hope for peace and prosperity throughout the continent.

Education & Science
Education and Science
Education in Germany is an area of mixed responsibility between the state governments and the central government. The <span style="color: #000000;">We</span><span style="color: #DD0000;">im</span><span style="color: #FFCC00;">ar</span> Constitution claims that schools are the domain of the central government, but in practice much of it is left to the states, as the Reichstag has historically been unable to pass an education law. Currently, with our control of Prussia and the Chancellorship, we can try to set policy for a large part of the country.

In a meeting with Chancellor BrÃ¼ning, Defense Minister Groener, General Schleicher, Finance Minister and Vice-Chancellor Hermann Dietrich, and our own Carl Severing, discussions focused on the upcoming presidential election and the organization of an effective propaganda campaign for Hindenburg. Severing outlined the threat that <span style="color: #7A3C00;">Nazism</span>âparticularly the <span style="color: #7A3C00;">
SA
</span>âposed to state authority and emphasized the election's critical role in keeping the <span style="color: #7A3C00;">Nazis</span> in check.
All present agreed that a decisive victory for Hindenburg and a campaign free from violent disturbances would greatly accelerate Germanyâs economic recovery and domestic political stabilization. Inter-Party Relationships
The <span style="color: #c00000;">
SPD
</span> cannot govern by itself yet. Because we do not have an electoral majority, we need the support of other parties. Recently, our governments have consisted of the <span style="color: #000000;">We</span><span style="color: #DD0000;">im</span><span style="color: #FFCC00;">ar</span> Coalition of the <span style="color: #c00000;">
SPD

State
</span>
<span style="color: #FFCC00;">
Liberal People's
</span>
 parties. 
However the formation of the <span style="color: #000000;">
</span> has only really made cooperation with the <span style="color: #D3C24D;">liberals</span> possible. 
 We can reach out and build additional connections with these parties, or perhaps we can stand alone.
